description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just received a copy of a production log file located at:

/home/user/logs/server.log

Your goal is to quickly isolate the non-404 ERROR events and prepare a short CSV summary for management.  
Follow these exact requirements:

1. Create a new directory exactly at:
/home/user/analysis

2. From /home/user/logs/server.log extract every line that  
   a) contains the literal word “ERROR” (uppercase) and  
   b) does NOT contain the substring “ERROR 404”.  

   Save the matching lines in the file:
/home/user/analysis/filtered_errors.log

   • The file must contain *only* the matching log lines, each on its own line, in the same order they appeared in the original log.  
   • Do not add any blank lines before, between, or after the data.

3. From filtered_errors.log produce a CSV summary file called:
/home/user/analysis/error_counts.csv

   • The CSV must have exactly four lines: one header line followed by three data lines.  
   • Header line must be:  
     error_code,count  
   • Each data line must contain the numeric error code (the number that follows the word “ERROR” in the log line) and the number of times that code appears in filtered_errors.log.  
   • The data lines **must be ordered by descending count**. If two codes have the same count, keep the lower numeric code first.  
   • No extra spaces are allowed anywhere in the file.  
   • End the file with a trailing newline so automated checks using wc -l pass.

Expected final layout:

/home/user
├── analysis
│   ├── error_counts.csv   (4 lines: header + 3 data rows)
│   └── filtered_errors.log (the raw matching log lines)
└── logs
    └── server.log         (the original log already provided)

You should need no more than 3–4 typical shell commands (e.g., mkdir, grep, awk, sort, uniq, etc.) to accomplish everything. Once complete, both new files must exactly match the required content and locations so automated tests that read them byte-for-byte will pass.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. Directory /home/user/logs exists (permissions 755).
2. File /home/user/logs/server.log (permissions 644) with the **exact** 11-line content below, each line terminated by a single Unix newline (LF):

2023-11-25 10:01:02 INFO User logged in
2023-11-25 10:02:15 ERROR 500 Internal Server Error at /login
2023-11-25 10:03:47 WARN Disk space low
2023-11-25 10:04:01 ERROR 404 Page Not Found /favicon.ico
2023-11-25 10:05:19 ERROR 500 Internal Server Error at /api/data
2023-11-25 10:06:33 ERROR 503 Service Unavailable at /reports
2023-11-25 10:07:45 INFO Job completed
2023-11-25 10:08:59 ERROR 500 Internal Server Error at /dashboard
2023-11-25 10:09:14 ERROR 503 Service Unavailable at /login
2023-11-25 10:10:21 ERROR 404 Page Not Found /robots.txt
2023-11-25 10:12:30 ERROR 502 Bad Gateway at /metrics

Desired state after successful task completion:

1. Directory /home/user/analysis exists (permissions 755).
2. File /home/user/analysis/filtered_errors.log (permissions 644) with **exactly** the following 6 lines, each terminated by a single LF:

2023-11-25 10:02:15 ERROR 500 Internal Server Error at /login
2023-11-25 10:05:19 ERROR 500 Internal Server Error at /api/data
2023-11-25 10:06:33 ERROR 503 Service Unavailable at /reports
2023-11-25 10:08:59 ERROR 500 Internal Server Error at /dashboard
2023-11-25 10:09:14 ERROR 503 Service Unavailable at /login
2023-11-25 10:12:30 ERROR 502 Bad Gateway at /metrics

3. File /home/user/analysis/error_counts.csv (permissions 644) with **exactly** the following 4 lines, each terminated by a single LF:

error_code,count
500,3
503,2
502,1

Automated verification logic:

• Check that /home/user/analysis directory exists.  
• Byte-compare /home/user/analysis/filtered_errors.log against the 6-line ground truth above.  
• Byte-compare /home/user/analysis/error_counts.csv against the 4-line ground truth above.  
• Confirm that both files have a trailing newline (wc -l == 6 and 4 respectively).  
If all comparisons pass, the task is considered complete.
